description: Add an optional 'summary' field to tool definitions across the SDK, shared types, MCP server pipeline, browser extension side panel, browser tools, and catalog generation. The summary is a short human-readable description shown in the side panel UI, while the full description remains for AI clients.

userStories.title: Add summary field to ToolDefinition in plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to provide an optional short summary for each tool so that the side panel shows a human-readable description instead of the full AI-facing description.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolDefinition interface in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts has an optional `summary?: string` field
The field is documented with a JSDoc comment explaining it is a short human-readable summary for the UI, distinct from description which is for AI clients
defineTool passes the field through unchanged (it already returns config as-is)
Existing plugins without summary continue to compile and work (field is optional)

userStories.title: Add summary field to shared types (ManifestTool, WireToolDef, ConfigStateBrowserTool, BrowserToolMeta)
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the summary field to flow through the entire wire protocol so it reaches the browser extension side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: ManifestTool in platform/shared/src/index.ts has optional `summary?: string`
WireToolDef in platform/shared/src/index.ts has optional `summary?: string`
ConfigStateBrowserTool in platform/shared/src/index.ts has optional `summary?: string`
BrowserToolMeta in platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts has optional `readonly summary?: string`
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/shared/src/index.ts`

Add `summary?: string` to three interfaces:
1. `ManifestTool` (lines 136-151) — after `description`
2. `WireToolDef` (lines 200-215) — after `description`
3. `ConfigStateBrowserTool` (lines 327-335) — after `description`

File: `platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts`
4. `BrowserToolMeta` interface — add `readonly summary?: string` after `description`

Note: Do NOT regenerate the catalog file contents yet (that happens in US-006 after browser tools get summaries). Just update the TypeScript interface so the type is ready. The existing catalog entries without `summary` will remain valid since the field is optional.

userStories.title: Thread summary through plugin build, MCP server loader, and extension wire protocol
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the summary field to be serialized during plugin build, validated during loading, passed through to the extension via WebSocket, and parsed by the extension's message router.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs-plugin build serializes `summary` from ToolDefinition to tools.json when present
validateTools in platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts accepts optional summary string and passes it through to ManifestTool
serializePluginForExtension in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts passes summary through to WireToolDef when present
The config state builder passes summary through to ConfigStateBrowserTool for browser tools
validatePluginPayload in platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts extracts and maps the summary field from incoming WebSocket payloads
The CachedBrowserTool type (or equivalent) in mcp-setup.ts carries the summary field
userStories.notes:
Multiple files to edit:

1. `platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts` — In `generateToolsManifest` (line 584-597), add summary to the returned object:
   ```ts
   ...(tool.summary ? { summary: tool.summary } : {}),
   ```
   Place it after `description`.

2. `platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts` — In `validateTools` (lines 133-200), add optional summary extraction alongside the other optional fields:
   ```ts
   const summary = typeof toolRecord.summary === 'string' ? toolRecord.summary : undefined;
   ```
   And include it in the pushed ManifestTool: `...(summary ? { summary } : {}),`

3. `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts` — In `serializePluginForExtension` (lines 108-156), the tools mapping needs:
   ```ts
   ...(t.summary ? { summary: t.summary } : {}),
   ```
   Also in the config state builder (around line 310-319) for browser tools:
   ```ts
   ...(ct.summary ? { summary: ct.summary } : {}),
   ```

4. `platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts` — The `CachedBrowserTool` type (check the interface, likely around line 75-85) needs a `summary?: string` field. In `rebuildCachedBrowserTools` (lines 86-98), add:
   ```ts
   ...(bt.summary ? { summary: bt.summary } : {}),
   ```

5. `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` — In `validatePluginPayload` (lines 162-235), the tool mapping inside `.map()` needs:
   ```ts
   ...(typeof t.summary === 'string' ? { summary: t.summary } : {}),
   ```

6. `platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts` — Add `summary?: string` to the `BrowserToolDefinition` interface (lines 18-27).

userStories.title: Display summary in side panel ToolRow tooltip and inline description
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to show the short summary in the tool row and tooltip instead of the full AI-facing description, falling back to description when no summary is provided.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolRow component accepts an optional `summary` prop
The inline description text (text-xs line under displayName) shows `summary ?? description`
When no summary is provided, the behavior is identical to before (description is shown)
BrowserToolsCard passes the summary field to ToolRow
ToolRow.stories.tsx is updated with stories showing both with-summary and without-summary states
userStories.notes:
Files to edit: 1. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx` — Add optional `summary?: string` to the props. Use `summary ?? description` for both the inline text and the tooltip: ```tsx const displayDescription = summary ?? description; // ...
{displayDescription}
// ... {displayDescription} ``` 2. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx` — In the ToolRow rendering (lines 241-250 and 255-264), pass `summary={tool.summary}`. 3. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx` — In the ToolRow rendering (lines 213-224 and 228-237), the BrowserToolState type needs to carry summary. Check the bridge.ts types to ensure BrowserToolState includes summary. Pass `summary={tool.summary}` to ToolRow. 4. Check `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts` — The `BrowserToolState` type likely mirrors `ConfigStateBrowserTool`. Ensure it includes `summary?: string`. Also check `WireToolDef` usage in the bridge. 5. `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.stories.tsx` — Add stories: `WithSummary` (showing a short summary), `WithoutSummary` (showing fallback to description), and a comparison story showing both side by side.

userStories.title: Add summary to all 40 browser tool definitions
userStories.description: As a user, I want all built-in browser tools to have short human-readable summaries in the side panel instead of verbose AI-facing descriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: All 40 browser tool definitions in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/*.ts have a `summary` field
Each summary is 1 short sentence, under 80 characters, describing what the tool does for a human reader
The full `description` field is NOT changed — it remains the detailed AI-facing description
userStories.notes:
Edit all 40 files in `platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/`. Each file has a `defineBrowserTool({...})` call. Add a `summary` field after `description`. Examples:

- `click-element.ts`: `summary: 'Click an element on the page'`
- `type-text.ts`: `summary: 'Type text into an input field'`
- `screenshot-tab.ts`: `summary: 'Capture a screenshot of a tab'`
- `execute-script.ts`: `summary: 'Run JavaScript in a tab'`
- `get-tab-content.ts`: `summary: 'Extract visible text from a page'`
- `list-tabs.ts`: `summary: 'List all open browser tabs'`
- `enable-network-capture.ts`: `summary: 'Start capturing network traffic'`
- `plugin-list-tabs.ts`: `summary: 'List tabs matching a plugin'`
- `plugin-analyze-site.ts`: `summary: 'Analyze a site for plugin development'`
- `extension-get-state.ts`: `summary: 'Get extension internal state'`

For tools where the current description is already short and human-readable (e.g., 'Add an emoji reaction to a message'), the summary can match or be slightly shorter. The key difference is: description can be verbose for AI context, summary is always concise for UI display.

IMPORTANT: Many browser tool descriptions are already relatively short. Focus on tools with long descriptions (like `plugin_analyze_site`, `browser_execute_script`, `browser_export_har`, `browser_query_elements`) where the summary adds the most value. For tools with already-short descriptions, still add a summary for consistency — it can be the same text or slightly abbreviated.

userStories.title: Update browser tools catalog generation to include summary
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the auto-generated browser tools catalog to include the summary field so the side panel has summaries available at startup before the WebSocket connects.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts extracts and includes the summary field from browser tool definitions
The generated browser-tools-catalog.ts file includes summary entries for all tools
The generated file is regenerated and committed
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:

1. `scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts` — Two changes:
   a. The dynamic import type assertion (line 33-35) needs `summary?: string` added to the tool shape:
      ```ts
      const { browserTools } = (await import(barrelPath)) as {
        browserTools: readonly { name: string; description: string; summary?: string; icon?: string; group?: string }[];
      };
      ```
   b. In the entries mapping (lines 41-48), include summary:
      ```ts
      .map(t => ({
        name: t.name,
        description: t.description,
        ...(t.summary ? { summary: t.summary } : {}),
        icon: t.icon ?? 'globe',
        group: t.group,
      }))
      ```
   c. In the codegen output (lines 69-76), add the summary line:
      ```ts
      if (entry.summary) lines.push(`    summary: ${singleQuote(entry.summary)},`);
      ```

2. After editing the script, run it to regenerate the catalog:
   ```bash
   npx tsx scripts/generate-browser-tools-catalog.ts
   ```
   This regenerates `platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts` with the new summary entries.

Note: The `BrowserToolMeta` interface in the generated file was already updated in US-002 to include `readonly summary?: string`. The codegen just needs to populate the values.

This is the E2E checkpoint because browser tools show in the side panel at startup — E2E tests verify the side panel renders correctly.
